seoTitle: Google Docs Highlight Shortcut: How to Use It (2026)
metaDescription: Highlight text fast in Google Docs with the Ctrl+Alt+H or Command+Option+H keyboard shortcut, and clear highlights just as quickly when you're done.
intro: Manually clicking through the highlight color picker for every sentence slows down document review. This guide covers the keyboard shortcut that instantly applies your last-used highlight color, how to swap between colors from the toolbar, and how to clear highlights when you're finished marking up a document. It's a quick habit that speeds up editing and study sessions alike.

steps.heading: Select your target text
steps.body: Highlight the text string, sentence, or paragraph you want to mark using standard keyboard or mouse selection.

steps.heading: Use the keyboard shortcut
steps.body: Press Ctrl+Alt+H on Windows/ChromeOS or Command+Option+H on macOS to instantly apply your last-used highlight color.

steps.heading: Adjust your highlight color
steps.body: Open the Highlight color pen tool on the toolbar to swap between preset colors or set a custom hex tone.

steps.heading: Remove a highlight
steps.body: Select the highlighted text and set the background color to None to clear the marker without altering the underlying text.

steps.heading: Use color-coding for organization
steps.body: Assign different highlight colors to different categories, such as edits, questions, or key data, to keep large reviews organized.

faq.question: Does the highlight shortcut work the same on Chromebooks?
faq.answer: Yes, Ctrl+Alt+H works on ChromeOS the same way it does on Windows, applying your most recently used highlight color.

faq.question: Can I remove a highlight with a keyboard shortcut too?
faq.answer: There isn't a dedicated shortcut for removal; you'll need to select the text and manually set the background to None.

faq.question: Will highlights show up if I export my document as a PDF?
faq.answer: Yes, highlight colors are preserved when exporting to PDF or other common formats.

faq.question: Can I highlight text on the Google Docs mobile app?
faq.answer: Yes, though the keyboard shortcut won't apply on mobile; you'll use the highlight tool in the formatting menu instead.
